'''Jack Broadbent''' (born 1 November 2000) is an English professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|ce|so|fb}} or {{rlp|wg}}er for [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] in the [[Super League]].

He has previously played for the [[Leeds Rhinos]] and the [[Castleford Tigers]] in the [[Super League]]. He has spent time on loan from Leeds at [[Featherstone Rovers]] and the [[Batley Bulldogs]] in the [[RFL Championship]].

==Background==
Broadbent attended [[Bruntcliffe Academy]] in [[Morley, West Yorkshire]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.therhinos.co.uk/player/jack-broadbent/|title=Jack Broadbent Biography|publisher=Leeds Rhinos|access-date=14 June 2022}}</ref>

Broadbent played his amateur rugby league with Dewsbury Moor and Batley Boys.

==Career==
===Leeds Rhinos===
Broadbent made his Super League debut in round 14 of the [[Super League XXV results|2020 Super League season]] for Leeds against the [[Catalans Dragons]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/leeds-vs-catalans/teams/54288|title=Starting Lineups – Leeds vs Catalans|publisher=Sky Sports |date=30 September 2020}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.superleague.co.uk/article/1587/round-14-fixtures-confirmed|title=Round 14 fixtures confirmed|publisher=Sky Sports |date=29 September 2020}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/leeds-rhinos-6-catalans-dragons-34-youthful-rhinos-impress-spirited-display-against-dragons-2988868|title=Leeds Rhinos 6 Catalans Dragons 34: Youthful Rhinos impress in spirited display against Dragons |newspaper=[[Yorkshire Evening Post]] |first=Peter |last=Smith |date=30 September 2020}}</ref>

In round 11 of the [[2021 Super League season]], he scored two tries for Leeds in a 38–12 victory over Salford.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12204/12343301/leeds-coach-richard-agar-pleased-after-eventful-win-against-salford/|title=Leeds coach Richard Agar pleased after 'eventful' win against Salford|website=Skysports.com}}</ref> The following week, he scored four tries in a 48–18 victory over [[Leigh Centurions|Leigh]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/57659413|title= Leeds Rhinos 48–18 Leigh Centurions – Four-try Broadbent seals win for hosts|work= BBC Sport}}</ref>

=== Castleford Tigers ===
On 2 December 2022, [[Castleford Tigers]] announced the signing of Broadbent on a two-year deal.<ref>{{cite web |date=2 December 2022 |title=Jack Broadbent: Castleford Tigers sign ex-Leeds Rhinos back on two-year deal |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/63840294 |access-date=3 December 2022 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |date=2 December 2022 |title=Jack Broadbent joins the Tigers |url=https://castlefordtigers.com/article.php?id=8382 |access-date=3 December 2022 |website=Castleford Tigers}}</ref> He made his Castleford debut in round 1 against [[Hull F.C.|Hull FC]],<ref>{{cite web |date=19 February 2023 |title=Super League: Hull FC 32–30 Castleford Tigers – Away side fall just short of remarkable fightback |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/64668525 |access-date=25 February 2024 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref> and scored his first try in round 2 against [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]].<ref>{{cite web |last=Cartwright |first=Phil |date=26 February 2023 |title=Super League: Castleford Tigers 6–24 St Helens – club world champions claim win in domestic opener |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/64721685 |access-date=25 February 2024 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref> He enjoyed a strong individual campaign in a disappointing season and said, "I've got everything I wanted. I'm getting game time in Super League and feel like I'm fulfilling my potential and have the chance to showcase that."<ref>{{cite web |last=O'Brien |first=James |date=27 June 2023 |title='Everything I wanted': Jack Broadbent loving life at Castleford Tigers after getting chance to fulfil potential |url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/everything-i-wanted-jack-broadbent-loving-life-at-castleford-tigers-after-getting-chance-to-fulfil-potential-4197705 |access-date=25 February 2024 |website=The Yorkshire Post}}</ref> He made 26 appearances and scored 6 tries in total in the [[2023 Castleford Tigers season|2023 season]], featuring predominately as a {{rlp|ce}} or {{rlp|so}} as well as filling in as a {{rlp|fb|hk}}, or {{rlp|wg}}er.<ref name="RLRKC" />

==== Hull Kingston Rovers====
On 23 April 2024, it was announced that Broadbent had signed for [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] on a season-long loan for the remainder of 2024, prior to a permanent three-year deal next (2025) season.<ref>{{cite web|website=BBC Sport|date=23 April 2024|author=|title=Broadbent joins Hull KR in Cas swap deal|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/c72pv1kek3eo/c72pv1kek3eo|accessdate=11 September 2024}}{{dead link|date=September 2025|bot=medic}}{{cbignore|bot=medic}}</ref>
On 12 October 2024, Broadbent played in Hull Kingston Rovers [[2024 Super League Grand Final]] loss against Wigan.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/live/2024/oct/12/wigan-v-hull-kr-super-league-grand-final-2024-live-score-updates?page=with:block-670aaacb8f087ed1d5a8a350/|title=Wigan v Hull KR: Super League Grand Final 2024 – as it happened|website=www.theguardian.com|accessdate=17 October 2024}}</ref>
On 7 June 2025, Broadbent played in Hull Kingston Rovers 8–6 2025 Challenge Cup final victory over Warrington. It was the clubs first major trophy in 40 years.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/articles/cn0gnqllpxlo|title=In pictures: Hull KR Challenge Cup victory parade|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref> 
On 18 September Hull Kingston Rovers won the League Leaders Shield on the final game of the season were Broadbent started on the bench in victory over Warrington, Broadbent played a crucial part in Hull Kingston Rovers 1st place season in covering for the injured star centre Oliver Gildart.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/c39rzxxpyjeo|title=Super League: Hull KR 28–20 Warrington Wolves: Robins win League Leaders' Shield|date=18 September 2025|website=BBC Sport|accessdate=6 November 2025}}</ref>
